cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A wireless broadcast technology pioneered in the early 20th century that transmitted voice and music to mass audiences, unifying national cultures and later carrying propaganda during both world wars

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A heavier-than-air flying machine developed in the early 20th century that revolutionized long-distance passenger travel, global commerce, and modern warfare

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Portable wireless telephone technology that became globally ubiquitous after the 1990s, leapfrogging landline infrastructure in developing countries and transforming daily communication

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A global digital network built on packet switching and the World Wide Web protocols that dramatically accelerated the flow of information, commerce, and culture from the 1990s onward

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Energy generated by controlled fission of uranium or plutonium in reactors, adopted widely after WWII for electricity despite concerns raised by disasters such as Chernobyl and Fukushima

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An oral hormonal contraceptive approved in 1960 that gave women unprecedented control over reproduction, fueling second-wave feminism and reshaping family and labor patterns worldwide

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A mid-20th-century agricultural transformation that spread high-yield seed varieties, synthetic fertilizers, and mechanized irrigation across Latin America and Asia, dramatically boosting food output

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Crops whose DNA has been altered in a laboratory to resist pests, tolerate herbicides, or improve nutrition, controversial for their ecological, economic, and health implications

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Standardized steel boxes introduced in the 1950s that could move seamlessly between ships, trains, and trucks, slashing the cost of global trade and enabling the rise of export manufacturing

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Italian engineer who sent the first transatlantic wireless signal in 1901, founding long-distance radio communication and earning the 1909 Nobel Prize in Physics

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Scottish-American inventor credited with patenting the first practical telephone in 1876, laying the foundation for the global voice communications industry

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American bicycle-mechanic siblings who achieved the first controlled, powered airplane flight at Kitty Hawk in 1903, initiating the age of aviation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American nurse and activist who opened the first U.S. birth-control clinic in 1916 and championed women's reproductive rights, later helping fund development of the oral contraceptive

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American biologist who led the research team that synthesized the first hormonal oral contraceptive, revolutionizing family planning in the 1960s

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American agronomist whose dwarf wheat varieties launched the Green Revolution, credited with saving roughly a billion lives and earning the 1970 Nobel Peace Prize

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The long-term average pattern of temperature, precipitation, and wind over decades or centuries in a given region

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The short-term, day-to-day state of the atmosphere, including temperature, humidity, and storms, distinguished from climate by its immediacy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The spread of arid, barren conditions into previously productive land, accelerated by overgrazing, deforestation, and rising temperatures in regions such as the Sahel

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The large-scale clearing of forests, especially the Amazon and Southeast Asian rainforests, for timber, cattle ranching, or agribusiness, reducing biodiversity and carbon absorption

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Contamination of the atmosphere by smog, particulates, and greenhouse gases released by industry, vehicles, and power generation, a major global public-health issue

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Contamination of rivers, lakes, groundwater, and oceans by industrial waste, fertilizer runoff, sewage, and plastics, threatening drinking water and marine ecosystems

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Accumulation of synthetic polymer debris in the world's oceans that entangles wildlife, leaches chemicals, and enters the food chain as microplastics

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A vast gyre of floating plastic debris between California and Hawaii, one of the most visible symbols of modern ocean pollution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Disposable plastic items such as bags, straws, and bottles used briefly and discarded, a leading source of marine debris now banned or taxed in many countries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Atmospheric gases such as carbon dioxide, methane, and nitrous oxide that trap solar heat, the primary driver of modern climate change

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Long-term shifts in global temperature and weather patterns caused largely by human emissions of greenhouse gases since industrialization, producing more extreme storms, heatwaves, and melting ice

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An observable consequence of melting polar ice and thermal expansion of oceans that threatens low-lying nations such as Bangladesh, the Maldives, and Pacific island states

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A United Nations body established in 1988 that synthesizes scientific research on global warming and produces the assessment reports guiding international climate policy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Biological preparations that stimulate immune response against specific pathogens, eradicating or suppressing diseases such as smallpox, polio, and measles worldwide

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Medicines such as penicillin that kill or inhibit bacteria, dramatically reducing deaths from infection after their mass production in WWII

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A highly contagious viral disease declared eradicated worldwide by the WHO in 1980, the first human disease ever eliminated through global vaccination

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A mosquito-borne parasitic disease concentrated in tropical Africa, Asia, and Latin America that still kills hundreds of thousands each year despite global control efforts

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A bacterial lung disease that spread widely with urbanization and poverty, treatable with antibiotics but resurgent in drug-resistant forms in the late 20th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A waterborne bacterial infection causing severe dehydration, repeatedly epidemic in 19th-century port cities and still linked to poor sanitation in the developing world

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A global influenza pandemic at the end of WWI that killed an estimated 50 million people, spread rapidly by wartime troop movements

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A paralytic viral disease largely eliminated in most of the world by mid-20th-century vaccination campaigns led by the WHO and Rotary International

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A viral pandemic that emerged in the late 20th century, devastating sub-Saharan Africa and prompting global activism, antiretroviral drug access campaigns, and prevention education

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A hemorrhagic viral disease with periodic deadly outbreaks in West and Central Africa, notably the 2014 epidemic that prompted a WHO emergency response

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Chronic non-communicable illness, now the leading cause of death worldwide, driven by sedentary lifestyles, processed diets, and rising obesity rates

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A degenerative brain condition increasingly common in aging populations, driving demand for long-term care systems in wealthy nations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American medical researcher who developed the first successful inactivated polio vaccine in 1955, refusing to patent it so it could be distributed cheaply worldwide

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A U.S. economic policy of the 1980s built on tax cuts, deregulation, and reduced social spending, advancing free-market neoliberal ideology

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A British economic program of the 1980s that privatized state industries, weakened unions, and embraced free-market reforms under Margaret Thatcher

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The official ideology of the People's Republic of China under the Communist Party, adapted after 1978 to incorporate market mechanisms while retaining one-party rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Deng Xiaoping's label for China's reformed economy that combines state ownership of strategic industries with private enterprise, foreign investment, and export manufacturing

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A post-industrial economic model in which growth depends on information, services, software, and skilled labor rather than manufacturing output

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An economic system centered on manufacturing physical goods in factories, dominant in the West during industrialization and now concentrated in East and Southeast Asia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Founded in 1995 to replace GATT, this Geneva-based body adjudicates trade disputes and negotiates tariff reductions among its member states

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A 1994 agreement that eliminated most tariffs among the United States, Canada, and Mexico, deepening North American economic integration

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A regional economic bloc founded in 1967 promoting trade, investment, and political cooperation among Southeast Asian states such as Indonesia, Thailand, and Vietnam

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Large firms such as Coca-Cola, Toyota, and Nestle that operate production, sales, and supply chains across many countries, shaping globalization's flows of capital, labor, and culture

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: U.S. president (1981-1989) whose supply-side economic reforms, military buildup, and summits with Gorbachev helped end the Cold War

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British prime minister (1979-1990) known as the Iron Lady who privatized industry, broke the miners' strike, and championed free-market conservatism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Chilean general who seized power in 1973, imposed brutal authoritarian rule, and implemented radical free-market reforms guided by the Chicago Boys economists

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Chinese leader who launched the 'Reform and Opening Up' policy in 1978, creating special economic zones and unleashing decades of market-driven growth

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Bretton Woods institution founded in 1944 that lends hard currency to countries facing balance-of-payments crises, often imposing structural-adjustment conditions

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American tech entrepreneur who co-founded Facebook in 2004, whose platform now shapes global political discourse, media consumption, and surveillance capitalism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Brazilian football superstar (1940-2022) whose three World Cup titles and global fame helped soccer become the world's most widely followed sport

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Jamaican reggae musician whose songs such as 'One Love' and 'Redemption Song' spread Rastafarian spirituality and anti-colonial messages to a global audience

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American swimmer who won 23 Olympic gold medals, the most in history, symbolizing the globalized media spectacle of the modern Olympics

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Chinese microblogging platform launched in 2009 that functions as the country's heavily censored analogue to Twitter, reflecting globalization's mediation through national firewalls

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A social and economic ideology emphasizing the buying of goods and services as a measure of identity and well-being, intensifying globally after WWII

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The buying and selling of goods and services over the internet, transforming retail since the 1990s through platforms such as Amazon and Alibaba

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American online auction and marketplace company founded in 1995, one of the first major e-commerce platforms to enable peer-to-peer global trade

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American e-commerce and cloud-computing giant founded by Jeff Bezos in 1994, often cited as emblematic of globalization's disruption of traditional retail

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Chinese e-commerce conglomerate founded by Jack Ma in 1999, connecting global buyers with Chinese manufacturers and rivaling Amazon in scale

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Internationally recognized corporate names such as Nike, Apple, or McDonald's whose logos, styles, and marketing promote a shared globalized consumer identity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Interactive internet-based platforms such as Facebook, Twitter, and TikTok that reshape politics, activism, and daily life by enabling rapid user-generated content sharing

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British public-service broadcaster founded in 1922 that became one of the most influential global news organizations, broadcasting in dozens of languages

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A quadrennial men's international soccer tournament organized by FIFA since 1930, widely considered the most-watched sporting event on the planet

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An international soccer tournament first held in 1991 that has helped raise the global visibility of women's athletics and gender-equity campaigns in sport

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Revived in 1896 as a modern international athletic competition held every four years, now a flagship spectacle of globalized media and national prestige

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Jamaican musical genre that emerged in the late 1960s, blending ska, rhythm and blues, and Rastafarian spirituality and spreading globally through artists such as Bob Marley

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mumbai-centered Hindi-language film industry whose song-and-dance spectacles are exported to South Asia, the Middle East, Africa, and diaspora communities worldwide

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: African American journalist and activist who documented lynching in the post-Reconstruction South and helped found the NAACP, an early voice in modern civil rights

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: First prime minister and president of Ghana whose 1957 independence victory inspired African decolonization and Pan-Africanist thought

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Anti-apartheid leader imprisoned for 27 years who became South Africa's first Black president in 1994, symbolizing global reconciliation and human-rights struggle

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American Baptist minister and civil-rights leader whose nonviolent activism and 'I Have a Dream' speech helped secure the Civil Rights Act and Voting Rights Act

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Kenyan environmentalist who founded the Green Belt Movement, won the 2004 Nobel Peace Prize, and linked tree planting to women's rights and democracy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A 1948 document adopted by the UN General Assembly enumerating civil, political, economic, and cultural rights as a common standard for all nations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A social and political movement advocating equal rights for women, progressing through 19th-century suffrage, mid-20th-century liberation, and global third-wave activism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A francophone literary and cultural movement developed in the 1930s by writers such as Aime Cesaire and Leopold Senghor that celebrated African heritage and resisted colonial assimilation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An ideology and movement promoting political and cultural solidarity among peoples of African descent, championed by figures such as Marcus Garvey and Kwame Nkrumah

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A continental body founded in 1963 to promote African unity, decolonization, and cooperation, replaced in 2002 by the African Union

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The right to vote in political elections, expanded through long struggles to include working-class men, women, and racial minorities in the 19th and 20th centuries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Latin American Catholic movement emerging in the 1960s that read scripture as a call to confront poverty and political oppression, influencing social activism across the Global South

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A demographic measure of the share of a population able to read and write, rising sharply worldwide in the 20th century as a marker of human development

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Landmark U.S. legislation that outlawed discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin in employment, education, and public accommodations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Landmark U.S. legislation that banned discriminatory practices such as literacy tests at the polls, dramatically expanding African American political participation in the South

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: India's constitutional system of quotas in education and public employment for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and Other Backward Classes, intended to remedy historic caste discrimination

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An international environmental NGO founded in 1971 known for direct-action campaigns against whaling, nuclear testing, and deforestation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Kenyan grassroots organization founded by Wangari Maathai in 1977 that combined tree planting with women's empowerment and democratic activism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A global network founded in 1989 that certifies producers and importers committed to fair prices, safe labor, and sustainable practices for Global South goods such as coffee and cocoa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An intergovernmental body founded in 1945 to maintain international peace, replacing the League of Nations and providing a forum for global diplomacy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The foundational 1948 UN document often referenced by its short title, proclaiming a common global standard of civil, political, and social rights

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A permanent tribunal established in 2002 in The Hague to prosecute individuals for genocide, war crimes, and crimes against humanity when national courts cannot or will not act

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A U.S. government program created by President Kennedy in 1961 that sends American volunteers abroad to work on education, health, and development projects
